# 2026 CADILLAC XT5 MODEL REVIEW - Frisco, TX

![2026 Cadillac XT5 Exterior](https://crestcadillactx.bhaimg.com/website/cadillac/2026-xt5-exterior.jpg)

- [Explore Cadillac XT5 Inventory](https://www.crestcadillactx.com/for-sale/xt5.htm)

- [Compare Models](https://www.crestcadillactx.com/showroom/index.htm)

- [Online Car Buying](https://www.crestcadillactx.com/online-car-buying/index.htm)

## What's New for the 2026 Cadillac XT5?

The 2026 Cadillac XT5 receives only minor updates, with slight changes to features across each trim. The XT5 remains a standout for its stylish design, spacious cargo area, and comfortable interior. Drivers and passengers will find plenty of headroom and legroom in the 2026 XT5.

## What Are the 2026 XT5 Trims & Features?

The 2026 Cadillac XT5 lineup consists of three distinct trim levels.

The base Luxury trim starts the lineup with 18-inch alloy wheels and a convenient power liftgate. Inside, this trim offers amenities such as a proximity keyless entry system and push-button start. Standard comfort features include dual-zone climate control, heated front seats, and synthetic leather upholstery for both seating rows. On the technology front, the Luxury trim is equipped with an eight-inch infotainment touchscreen, wireless Android Auto® and Apple Carplay®, a Wi-Fi hotspot, and an eight-speaker Bose sound system.

The Premium Luxury trim upgrades to 20-inch wheels, power-folding side mirrors, and automatic wipers. It also adds a hands-free liftgate, panoramic sunroof, and ambient lighting. The Premium Luxury trim features a heated steering wheel, memory settings for the driver's seat, and leather upholstery. Technology upgrades include a wireless charging pad, navigation, and a 14-speaker Bose audio system.

The Sport trim is next and adds several performance features. It comes with a sport-tuned suspension, all-wheel drive, Brembo performance brakes with red calipers, a cooling system for towing, and adaptive cruise control.

![2026 Cadillac XT5 Interior](https://crestcadillactx.bhaimg.com/website/cadillac/2026-xt5-interior.jpg)

## How Much Horsepower Does the 2026 XT5 Have?

The 2026 XT5 Luxury and Premium Luxury trims are equipped with a turbocharged 2.0L four-cylinder engine producing 235 horsepower. The Sport trim features a 3.6L V6 engine with 310 horsepower, which is also available as an option on the Premium Luxury trim. Both engines use a nine-speed automatic transmission. All-wheel drive is standard on the Sport and available on other trims.

## What Safety Features Does the 2026 XT5 Include?

For safety, the 2026 XT5 includes forward collision warning, lane departure mitigation, front and rear parking sensors, a rear seat reminder, and cornering lights.

## Visit Us in Frisco Today

[Contact us](https://www.crestcadillactx.com/contact.htm) at Crest Cadillac today to learn more about the 2026 Cadillac XT5.

web.title: Bose Sound System OK but not Great - Considering Aftermarket ...
web.description: The Bose system in the ELR is not the world's best. I have found after having it for a year the speakers seem to have broken in and the system is punchier.
web.markdown:
[![Cadillac Owners Forum banner](https://images.platforum.cloud/banners/cadillacforums_com_banner_2880w.jpg?auto=webp&fit=bounds&format=pjgp&optimize=high&quality=50)](https://www.cadillacforums.com/)

Menu


[Log in](https://www.cadillacforums.com/login/)

* * *

[Sign up](https://www.cadillacforums.com/register/)

* * *

- [Home](https://www.cadillacforums.com/)
- [Forums](https://www.cadillacforums.com/forums/)
- [Cadillac Vehicle Discussions](https://www.cadillacforums.com/forums/#cadillac-vehicle-discussions.1)
- [EV and PHEV Cadillac Vehicles](https://www.cadillacforums.com/forums/ev-and-phev-cadillac-vehicles.1067/)
- [ELR Forum - 2014-2016](https://www.cadillacforums.com/forums/elr-forum-2014-2016.268/)

Spiffster

[S](https://www.cadillacforums.com/members/spiffster.973521/)

Im not terribly impressed with the sound system in this car. I purchased my Volt new with the base sound system (Not Bose) and spent nearly $2k on upgrades. Have Focal component, front and back with a JL hybrid (digital / analog) amp. I think it sounds slightly better than the Cadi for most music.

I have read that Bose systems are relatively proprietary and a bit trickier to modify. Has anyone bothered to upgrade, or are you all just sticking with the Bose system? I am considering transfer of equipment from the Volt (I still have) to the Cadi before I sell the Volt... thats assuming speakers will fit. I havent done a whole lot of research yet, but figured some here may have experience with the ELR sound system upgrade.

[#1](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16283185)· [Dec 30, 2016](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16283185)

Im not terribly impressed with the sound system in this car. I purchased my Volt new with the base sound system (Not Bose) and spent nearly $2k on upgrades. Have Focal component, front and back with a JL hybrid (digital / analog) amp. I think it sounds slightly better than the Cadi for most music.

I have read that Bose systems are relatively proprietary and a bit trickier to modify. Has anyone bothered to upgrade, or are you all just sticking with the Bose system? I am considering transfer of equipment from the Volt (I still have) to the Cadi before I sell the Volt... thats assuming speakers will fit. I havent done a whole lot of research yet, but figured some here may have experience with the ELR sound system upgrade.

Sort by
Oldest first


[#2](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16283769)· [Dec 30, 2016\\
\\
\\
(Edited)](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16283769)

Brilliant marketing on the part of Cadillac. You're pretty much limited to the OEM system, (and you are correct, Bose custom-configures their sound systems for the unique acoustic environment of each model) unless you're willing to give up something in return.

For example, the CUE system controls the amp via the HMI module. An aftermarket system would be controlled by the head unit. Where would you mount that? You can't pull the CUE faceplate out without affecting the other systems it controls. If you are able to find a LAN-compatible upgrade amp, will it be fully functional with the HMI firmware?

Configuring a replacement system to operate with the steering wheel-mounted controls can be problematic without a special adapter harness, depending on what components you eliminate.

With modern cars, where the integrated electronics are concerned, nothing is easy anymore.

[Google](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FQN37346?tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) "Aftermarket CUE-replacement sound systems." The results are . . . that's right. . . ZERO. Nada. Zip. Probably for the reasons cited above. Upgrades to the sound system aren't impossible - you could custom design an enclosure for the dash to fit a double-DIN head unit. . . but what about losing (all of) the other CUE-controlled functions?

Realistically, speaker replacement is your only option. But . . .

Bose speakers have a different impedance rating, so you can't just swap them out for standard 8 ohm speakers, which are half that, limiting your aftermarket choices.

Exclusivity has a price. . . For everything else, there's MasterCard!

CC

[#3](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16287057)· [Jan 1, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16287057)

True, the Bose system in the ELR is not the world's best. I have found after having it for a year the speakers seem to have "broken in" and the system is punchier. Hope that's the same for you. Happy 2107!

[#4](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16294473)· [Jan 4, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16294473)

I wonder if the Bose active noise cancellation is disabled if you upgrade the speakers...

[#5](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16297097)· [Jan 5, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16297097)

Seems like you have to start from scratch when upgrading Bose because of all the proprietary crap. I have come to the conclusion that its just not worth the effort to upgrade. Sounds just fine for most music.

[#6](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16461889)· [Mar 15, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16461889)

Sorry for the late response to the OP. I just picked up a used 2014 (silver/black, lux/ACC) and while I thought the Bose sounded better than the setup in my Volt and decent for a factory system I have some petty nice stuff in the house and I was missing that extra performance that I'm sure your previous aftermarket setup brought to the table. The first thing to consider is your source. FM/XM suffer from compression and the bandwith limitations of their respective technologies. I use [Tidal](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Tidal&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) lossless (CD quality, FLAC streaming) on my [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) but remember if you're streaming from your [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) to the Bose that the Bluetooth implementation is the limiting factor (Lossy CODEC - APT-X is better but not great and the ELR doesn't have it any way). The DAC and the headphone amplifier (designed with portability/power consumption and cost as primary concerns) in an [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) are also barriers to quality even coming straight of the headphone jack and into the AUX jack. As an experiment I dragged my portable OPPO HA-2 headphone amp (it's about the size of an [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) 6/7 overall) and stuck it in the storage console between the seats. I plugged the USB to [mini](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=mini&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) USB cord power cord into the HA-2 to provide it with juice and took the line level output into the AUX jack. I then connected the [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) 6 plus via a lightning to USB cable to the OPPO which now bypasses the internal DAC of the [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) and passes the bitstream to the HA-2 for decoding using its state of the art Sabre ESS 32bit DAC. Bingo! The soundstage opened up, the EQ could be taken all the way back to flat (and sounded great there), and the grittiness of the high end was gone. My guess is that the speaker setup and integrated amps along with the custom EQ that Bose engineered into the car are fine but the electronics/decoders/DACS in the front end leave something to be desired. Of course, you give up track control through the steering wheel (volume works fine) but the difference is dramatic enough for me to simply mount my [iPhone](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=iPhone&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) using a ProClip mount to the right of the center stack so I can control the music directly from the phone. The Oppo isn't cheap ($300) but it really shows off the Bose setup to its best. Hope that helps!

Tim

[#7](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16481545)· [Mar 22, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16481545)

Hi Tim,

Does the OPPO do anything to boost the overall richness of the music? I get to enjoy a wide range of factory systems and I really enjoy the tight punchy rich accuracy in [Acura](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Acura&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) ELS sound systems. The Bose system in my ELR just sounds kind of meh with an average soundstage. The ELS system has such a great presence where you can pinpoint instruments. Thanks!

[#8](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16494202)· [Mar 27, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16494202)

I have run live sound for 35 years or so (different application entirely!) and been an audio enthusiast since I was a freshman in high school (I'm 55 in May). Julian Hirsch at Stereo Review got me started and I have been around or owned a long string of great bang for the buck systems which included components from Harman Kardon, Yamaha, Denon, Onkyo, Sony ES, Polk, Nakamichi, JBL, Pioneer Elite and, more currently, Emotiva separates (BASX MC-700 A/V processor w/A-500 5-ch power amp, TA-100 Stereo Integrated Amp/DAC), GoldenEar speakers (Triton 3 powered towers, SuperCenter X, Aon 3 bookshelves), Marantz HD-AMP1, Polk TL3 speakers and a pair of OPPO PM-3 Planar Magnetic headphones to go with the OPPO HA-2 headphone amp. I share, certainly not to brag, but to reinforce the idea that while some of these components are not necessarily inexpensive, within their respective categories they are almost universally acclaimed as offering performance that is the equal to (and sometimes much better) competitors at the same and even many times the price and also to share my background and experience with audio, as modest as it may be.

For all the negatives associated with the Bose systems in most autos today (I've had them in an [Acura](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Acura&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) NSX, [Audi A6](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Audi+A6&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow), [Chevy Volt](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Chevy&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) and now the ELR) such as difficult to upgrade, unconventional speaker impedances, integrated amplifiers, etc., many of the limitations are driven by the auto manufacturer (read $$$) and not necessarily by the engineering that Bose brings to the OEMs. This appears to be an example of a perfectly acceptable custom EQ/speaker integration effort by Bose that was hobbled by the (far from state of the art) CUE system. While I am not intimately familiar with the specifications of the electronics used in the CUE system, my simple experiment of bypassing the CUE system as a source and feeding the output of what many regard to be a spectacular sounding DAC (Sabre ESS 32-bit - [Google](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FQN37346?tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) is your friend) into the AUX jack (which I'm assuming is fed more or less straight into the amplifiers integrated into the Bose speaker system, though I would note that the EQ and balance controls still work so there is a preamplifier/mixer stage ahead of the amps) which resulted in a dramatic improvement in the sound system.

To answer your question more directly, with well recorded material (think later Steely Dan/Donald \*\*\*en, Joey Alexander, Joe Henderson Big Band) the sound stage is expansive and as you describe with the Elliot Scheiner designed system (which I have not heard) in the [Acura](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Acura&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow), instruments are clearly placed across the soundstage. In addition, the need for EQ is greatly diminished. Well recorded material can be played flat and yet there is plenty of "air" and "punch" in the system with a very smooth high-end. I have an EQ preset that is two or three bumps of the bass and treble (midrange is flat) that I will dial in for less well recorded material (I listen to a lot of jazz from the 50's and 60's and sometimes it can use the help) but nothing like I needed to do when listening to my phone ( [Tidal](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Tidal&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow)) via Bluetooth and the CUE system. There is no way I will use the CUE system by itself for audio. For $300 you can't go wrong sonically and other than the loss of steering mounted controls for track selection and "now playing" info on the center stack (which I can see on my mounted phone) and the need to turn the HA-2 off and on in the glove box every time I enter or exit the car (or it's battery will run down when the car is not on) given the obvious increase in performance that the setup brings. Many (most) people may not hear the difference and not find it worth the hassle but I do. OPPO has a 30 day money-back guarantee. Try it and if the improvement in sound isn't worth the slight hassle then send it back. Hope that helps! Good luck and if you try it please let me know how it works for you.

Tim

P.S. I have no financial interest in OPPO/Bose etc.

[#9](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16497786)· [Mar 29, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16497786)

Thanks so much for the info Tim. I'm going to give it a try in a couple of weeks once i get a mountain of work off my plate. I'l let you know. Cheers!

[#10](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16526801)· [Apr 11, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16526801)

Huge thanks for the informative and detailed responses, Tim. This does make a great deal of sense and I went ahead and ordered a similar product. I ordered a Dragonfly Red, and once it arrives I will report back. Unfortunately the more I switch between cars (Volt with upgrades vs stock ELR) the more I think the Bose system sucks. I also recently signed up to [Tidal](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Tidal&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) and hearing the noticeable jump in sound quality in my Volt vs no noticeable improvement in the ELR has exacerbated the issue. Hopefully this will help.

[#11](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16531922)· [Apr 13, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16531922)

Hi all,

I gave the Oppo a try but my ears heard little to no change in the sound. It will be going back. Worth a try though.

[#12](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16539002)· [Apr 17, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16539002)

Sorry it didn't work out for you...

Best,

Tim

[#13](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16542474)· [Apr 18, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16542474)

I received my Dragonfly Red on Saturday and just got a chance to tune things on my ELR. I must say Im pretty impressed... I had to do some configuring to get hifi from [Tidal](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=Tidal&tag=vs-pl-cadillacforums-com-20&rel=nofollow) via USB Audio Pro app. Anyway, some tracks sound amazing while others are "just" good. For example one track (Sasha Lazard - Awakening) which sounds amazing on just about any system, went all ho-hum. I mean, it sounded good but was relatively flat compared to other music which took on an extra dimension. A few tunes damn near put me into a coma they sounded so epic. Mixed bag so far... Overall happy with the purchase though. I will say, I am kinda afraid to plug this thing into my Volt's Focal system. If I get the same level of accentuation, I may once again feel the Bose system is inadequate.

[#14](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16612866)· [May 16, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16612866)

Can someone with a CD player installed verify whether the sound is better with CD compared to compressed XM, FM? If markedly better it could be best to just spend money on installing the CD for music.

[#15](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16615114)· [May 17, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16615114)

I have been exclusively listening to satellite and it sounds marginal at best. I happened to put some cd's in iTunes library(recorded in lossless). All I did was play it through Bluetooth and wow the system sounded dramatically different !!! I had a GREAT backroad solo drive on a sunny Dallas day rocking out.

[#16](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16615370)· [May 17, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16615370)

XM radio for music is extremely poor audio. It is nice to have and I use it because it is convenient but I realize I am not hearing quality sound AT ALL. It has to be that way because it is so clipped down highs and lows and everything else to be small enough to stream over the satellite.

So if you are complaining of poor Bose in the ELR and using XM to decide you should consider the poor input source before you judge. And putting an aftermarket audio system behind same XM is going to also provide poor quality audio. Could spend a million dollars on the best amplification but if you amplify a clipped up low quality source it still won't sound as good as better quality input.

That is why I asked about using the CD drive because you can put through a much much much better quality source input to hear through the Bose.

0Reply

[#17](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16625562)· [May 21, 2017](https://www.cadillacforums.com/threads/bose-sound-system-ok-but-not-great-considering-aftermarket-upgrade.928609/#post-16625562)

ANYTHING sounds better than XM, that's awful stuff. Mine has the CD player. There's an edge to CDs that discriminating ears can hear over plugging in an iPod. I wouldn't call it a huge difference though.

web.title: Cadillac XT5 Trim Levels Overview
web.description: This option offers Marianna drivers with top-shelf features like a turbocharged engine, Bose premium sound system, and automatic emergency ...
web.markdown:
# Cadillac XT5 Trim Levels Overview

December 31st, 2024 by[Bill Cramer Cadillac](https://www.billcramercadillac.com/author/billcramercadillac_author/ "Posts by Bill Cramer Cadillac")

Share this Post:
Share on FacebookShare on Twitter

![2025 Cadillac XT5 lineup in desert](https://dealerinspire-image-library-prod.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/images/qXzjmj7XZm044IX5qJcVXGci5Ai8eQ6fEeQIpHeD.jpg)

Are you shopping around Tallahassee for a Cadillac XT5 SUV? Then, you will need to decide whether you prefer the Cadillac XT5 Luxury, Cadillac XT5 Premium Luxury, or Cadillac XT5 Sport trims. Take a moment to learn about the differences between each of these Cadillac XT5 trim levels when it comes to pricing and features. Then, [complete a secure financing application](https://www.billcramercadillac.com/finance/apply-for-financing/) and head to Bill Cramer Cadillac for a test drive in Panama City.

[View Inventory](https://www.billcramercadillac.com/new-vehicles/) [Contact Us](https://www.billcramercadillac.com/contactusform/)

## 2025 Cadillac XT5 Trim Levels: MSRP & Highlights

You will find feature highlights and MSRPs for each of the three XT5 trims that are available for the 2025 model year below. Please review them at your convenience, and contact us if you have any questions.

### 2025 Cadillac XT5 Luxury (FWD/AWD): $44,295\*

First up among the Cadillac XT5 trim levels is the Cadillac XT5 Luxury trim. This option offers Marianna drivers with top-shelf features like a turbocharged engine, Bose premium sound system, and automatic emergency braking system right out of the gate. Highlights include:

- 2.0L Turbo 4-cylinder engine
- 18-inch multi-spoke alloy wheels with Bright Silver finish
- Bose® premium eight-speaker system
- Front-wheel drive
- Bright Brushed Aluminum roof rails
- Automatic emergency braking and lane keep assist with lane departure warning
- Front and rear park assist
- Dual-zone automatic climate control with individual climate settings for driver and front passenger

### 2025 Cadillac XT5 Premium Luxury (FWD/AWD): $50,595\*

Step up to the Cadillac XT5 Premium Luxury, and you will enjoy the addition of a far more dynamic sound system with 14 speakers, as well as wireless phone charging, and more. Let us take a look at some highlights:

- All Luxury standard features, plus:
- 20-inch six-split spoke alloy wheels with Polished/Android finish
- Bose Performance Series 14-speaker system with Cadillac speaker grille pattern
- Memory Package with two “presets” for power driver seat, outside mirrors, and power tilt and telescoping steering column
- Wireless phone charging
- Hands-free power liftgate with programmable memory height and Cadillac-branded projection light
- Illuminating door handles
- Leather seating surfaces with mini-perforated inserts
- Side blind zone alert and rear cross-traffic alert

### 2025 Cadillac XT5 Sport (AWD): $57,195\*

The ultimate option among the Cadillac XT5 trim levels, the Sport comes equipped with a standard V6 engine, wheel upgrades, sport-tuned brakes, performance-enhanced drivetrain, and more. Standout features are as follows:

- All Luxury and Premium Luxury features, plus:
- 3.6L V6 engine
- Standard AWD
- 20-inch 12-spoke alloy wheels with Pearl Nickel finish
- Twin clutch sport control all-wheel drive
- Brembo® Performance front brakes and front and rear red-painted calipers
- Gloss Black roof rails

## Test-Drive Cadillac XT5 Trim Levels Near Destin

The best way to compare the Cadillac XT5 Luxury, Cadillac XT5 Premium Luxury, and Cadillac XT5 Sport is to test-drive them all at our Panama City showroom. Simply head over to experience the XT5 trims in action. If you have any questions, call Bill Cramer Cadillac at **(850) 387-4030**. But before you head out, read our overview of the 2025 Cadillac XT5 exterior features and Cadillac XT5 engine specs.

Posted in [Model Features](https://www.billcramercadillac.com/category/model-features/)
